behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: CSV parser/row index tracking -> schema validator -> DLQ writer. A unit test of validator logic alone would miss row-number propagation bugs. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and no AI/ML dependency exists, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline in-process with a small CSV fixture and local test doubles for sinks; assert rows missing required fields are excluded from normalized output and emitted to DLQ with exact original row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is a local transform rule on input strings to canonical output; no module boundary is required to expose the stated failure. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets conversion correctness itself, not parser/DB/DLQ handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous dates) and expected canonical outputs.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: The failure depends on real runtime behavior (process memory growth, throughput, timeout under load). In-process mocks can hide allocator/GC/backpressure effects, so REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push to System.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a 500k-row fixture, enforce the production timeout setting, and assert completion before timeout plus peak RSS below a defined memory ceiling.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about real external contract handling (S3 503 semantics, SDK error mapping, retry timing). A pure mock can falsely pass if real 503 responses are surfaced differently. Rejection-gate: Integration is rejected because behavior text explicitly anchors to real S3 transient 503 behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3-compatible endpoint/test harness that returns one or more real HTTP 503 responses before success; verify retry count and increasing backoff intervals, and that batch processing ultimately succeeds.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Restart mid-batch is an OS/process-boundary durability problem (checkpoint/transaction/idempotency across process restarts). Integration can test idempotency logic, but real restart semantics can still fail, so minimum is System.
behaviors.test_description: Start processing a sizable batch against a real PostgreSQL instance, terminate the pipeline process mid-run, restart it with the same input/checkpoint state, then assert no duplicate rows (by business key) and full eventual completion.
